info.title: authz-admin
info.description: Cross-service authorization admin BFF (Phase 7). Aggregates `/api/access_policies/*` surfaces across consumer services (realtime, filez, ...) so a single operator UI can answer 'who can see what + why' without per-service tabs.

post.description: Forward a 'who can see X?' query to a single upstream consumer and return its response verbatim. Frontend fans out by calling this once per upstream tab in parallel.

400.description: Unknown upstream / oversize body / missing-field request

401.description: Missing identity header (Authorization or x-realtime-user-id or x-filez-user-id)

post.description: Forward an explain query to a single upstream consumer and return its response verbatim. Frontend fans out by calling this once per upstream in parallel.

400.description: Unknown upstream / oversize body / missing-field request

401.description: Missing identity header (Authorization or x-realtime-user-id or x-filez-user-id)

post.description: Forward a granted-apps query to one upstream consumer. The frontend fans out per upstream tab.

400.description: Unknown upstream / oversize body / missing-field request

post.description: Forward a bulk-revoke-by-app to one upstream consumer.

400.description: Unknown upstream / invalid uuid / oversize body

post.description: Forward an audit-log query to a single upstream consumer and return its response verbatim. Frontend fans out by calling this once per upstream tab.

400.description: Unknown upstream / oversize body / missing-field request

401.description: Missing identity header (Authorization or x-realtime-user-id or x-filez-user-id)

get.description: Process liveness probe. Returns 200 once axum is serving — does NOT verify upstream reachability (that's `/api/upstreams`).
List configured upstreams with a live reachability probe. The first thing the admin UI calls; drives which per-consumer panels render.

ApiResponseStatus.description:
Matches realtime-server's wire shape: `Success` serialises to
the bare string `"Success"`; `Error("...")` serialises to
`{"Error": "..."}`. Keeping the shape identical means the
`@my-own-web-services/react-components` envelope parser
doesn't need a per-service branch.

ApiResponse_AuditLogResponse.description:
Standard envelope shared across MOWS services. authz-admin uses
the same shape as realtime / filez so frontend code that lives
in `@my-own-web-services/react-components` can keep one parser.

upstream_body.description:
Verbatim JSON the upstream returned. Shape:
`{status, message, data: {entries: [...], next_cursor?}}`
for 200; the upstream's error envelope otherwise.

upstream_status.description:
HTTP status the upstream returned. Surfaced so the frontend
can distinguish 200 (page) / 403 (no such resource OR not
yours) / 401 (no identity) without re-parsing the upstream
envelope.

ApiResponse_ByResourceResponse.description:
Standard envelope shared across MOWS services. authz-admin uses
the same shape as realtime / filez so frontend code that lives
in `@my-own-web-services/react-components` can keep one parser.

upstream_body.description:
Verbatim JSON the upstream returned. Shape:
`{status, message, data: {resource_owner_id, policies}}`
for 200; the upstream's error envelope otherwise.

upstream_status.description:
HTTP status the upstream returned. Surfaced so the frontend
can distinguish 200 (owner sees policies) / 403 (not the
owner, or no such resource — deliberately collapsed by the
upstreams) / 401 (no identity) without re-parsing the
upstream envelope.

ApiResponse_ExplainResponse.description:
Standard envelope shared across MOWS services. authz-admin uses
the same shape as realtime / filez so frontend code that lives
in `@my-own-web-services/react-components` can keep one parser.

upstream_body.description:
Verbatim JSON the upstream returned. After review-1 R4
both realtime + filez emit `{ data: { evaluations: [...] } }`
in the response, so the frontend just walks the envelope.
A stale upstream that still uses the old `auth_evaluations`
name will surface as the SPA's empty-state placeholder —
the wire_shape_guard unit tests in each upstream pin the
canonical name to catch drift before deploy.

upstream_status.description:
HTTP status the upstream returned. The BFF surfaces it
so the frontend can distinguish 200 / 403 / 5xx without
re-parsing the upstream envelope.

ApiResponse_GrantedAppsResponse.description:
Standard envelope shared across MOWS services. authz-admin uses
the same shape as realtime / filez so frontend code that lives
in `@my-own-web-services/react-components` can keep one parser.

upstream_body.description:
Verbatim JSON from the upstream — shape:
`{status, message, data: {apps: [{app_id, policy_count}]}}`
on 200.

ApiResponse_HealthResponse.description:
Standard envelope shared across MOWS services. authz-admin uses
the same shape as realtime / filez so frontend code that lives
in `@my-own-web-services/react-components` can keep one parser.

ApiResponse_RevokeByAppResponse.description:
Standard envelope shared across MOWS services. authz-admin uses
the same shape as realtime / filez so frontend code that lives
in `@my-own-web-services/react-components` can keep one parser.

ApiResponse_UpstreamsResponse.description:
Standard envelope shared across MOWS services. authz-admin uses
the same shape as realtime / filez so frontend code that lives
in `@my-own-web-services/react-components` can keep one parser.

cursor.description:
Opaque cursor from the prior page's `next_cursor`.
Forwarded verbatim; upstream rejects malformed values with
a clean 400.

limit.description: Page size. Forwarded verbatim — upstream clamps.

resource_id.description:
Optional resource id. Typed as Uuid at the BFF so a malformed
input is a clean 400 from the BFF instead of a 500/parse
error from the upstream.

resource_type.description:
Optional resource type — when present alongside
`resource_id`, the response is scoped to events on that
resource (owner-only). When absent, the upstream returns
the caller's own actions.

upstream.description:
Which upstream to forward to. Must match a `key` from
`/api/upstreams` (`"realtime"`, `"filez"`).

upstream_body.description:
Verbatim JSON the upstream returned. Shape:
`{status, message, data: {entries: [...], next_cursor?}}`
for 200; the upstream's error envelope otherwise.

upstream_status.description:
HTTP status the upstream returned. Surfaced so the frontend
can distinguish 200 (page) / 403 (no such resource OR not
yours) / 401 (no identity) without re-parsing the upstream
envelope.

resource_id.description:
The id of the resource to inspect. Deserialized as a `Uuid`
so an invalid input (e.g. "not-a-uuid") becomes a clean BFF
400 with a typed message instead of a 500 from the upstream's
parse error. Review R9.

resource_type.description:
Upstream-specific resource type identifier — passed through
to the upstream's `/api/access_policies/by_resource`
verbatim. Realtime accepts `"Channel"` etc., filez accepts
`"File"` / `"FileGroup"`.

upstream.description:
Which upstream to forward to. Must match a `key` from
`/api/upstreams` (`"realtime"`, `"filez"`).

upstream_body.description:
Verbatim JSON the upstream returned. Shape:
`{status, message, data: {resource_owner_id, policies}}`
for 200; the upstream's error envelope otherwise.

upstream_status.description:
HTTP status the upstream returned. Surfaced so the frontend
can distinguish 200 (owner sees policies) / 403 (not the
owner, or no such resource — deliberately collapsed by the
upstreams) / 401 (no identity) without re-parsing the
upstream envelope.

action.description:
Upstream-specific action identifier — passed through
verbatim.

resource_type.description:
Upstream-specific resource type identifier — passed through
to the upstream's `/api/access_policies/explain` verbatim.

upstream.description:
Which upstream to forward to. Must match a `key` from
`/api/upstreams` (`"realtime"`, `"filez"`).

upstream_body.description:
Verbatim JSON the upstream returned. After review-1 R4
both realtime + filez emit `{ data: { evaluations: [...] } }`
in the response, so the frontend just walks the envelope.
A stale upstream that still uses the old `auth_evaluations`
name will surface as the SPA's empty-state placeholder —
the wire_shape_guard unit tests in each upstream pin the
canonical name to catch drift before deploy.

upstream_status.description:
HTTP status the upstream returned. The BFF surfaces it
so the frontend can distinguish 200 / 403 / 5xx without
re-parsing the upstream envelope.

upstream_body.description:
Verbatim JSON from the upstream — shape:
`{status, message, data: {apps: [{app_id, policy_count}]}}`
on 200.

context_app_id.description:
Typed as Uuid at the BFF deserializer — same R9 / SEC-5
stance as the other forwarders: malformed input becomes a
clean 400 from the BFF, never reaches the upstream.

reachable.description:
Whether the upstream answered its `/api/health` with 200
at the moment of the probe. The probe is fired in parallel
per upstream; total response time is bounded by the
slowest upstream (or its timeout — 10 s, set in
`AppState::new`).
